steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The decisive phrase is “though,” which introduces a contrast between focusing on Brooks's immediate surroundings versus her broad literary influences. This pivotal word signals that the correct answer must accommodate this transition appropriately.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: C captures the shift with a semicolon, correctly introducing “though” mid-sentence as a contrast marker: IC; though, IC. A, B, and D either incorrectly separate independent clauses or misplace the punctuation around the transitional word. That matches choice C — C is correct.
